topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound bled through the cheap headphones, a familiar melody that used to feel like a triumphant declaration. Now, it just felt like an accusation. I was nursing lukewarm coffee in my cramped studio, the silence amplifying the intrusion. It was my song, the one I poured every sleepless night into, and it was being played by some stranger down the street, stripped of its nuance, turned into something saccharine and utterly hollow. My jaw tightened, a muscle jumping beneath my skin. I slowly pulled the headphones off, the sudden quiet feeling like a physical weight settling on my chest. I stood up, pacing the worn floorboards, my movements sharp and jerky. I needed to go out there, to confront the mediocrity that was drowning my creation.

[story 2]

I watched the grainy video on my phone screen, the familiar opening chords grating on my nerves. It was the same arrangement, the same clumsy interpretation, played by someone who clearly had no soul to give the music. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. I clenched my fists so hard my knuckles turned white, the tension radiating through my arms. "Amateur," I muttered under my breath, the word tasting like ash. I slammed my laptop shut, the noise echoing in the small room. I began furiously deleting files, a desperate attempt to erase the existence of that performance, to wipe the slate clean before the lingering sound could poison whatever fragile sense of pride I had left.

[story 3]

The air in the bar was thick with cheap beer and forced enthusiasm. Then, it started. My track. But the way they played it—all those clumsy alterations, the wrong emphasis on the bridge—it was a deliberate insult. A cold knot formed in my stomach, tightening with every discordant note. I kept my gaze fixed on the stage, refusing to look at the performer, but I could feel the scrutiny, the way they were basking in the attention I had labored to create. I took a long, slow sip of my drink, the liquid burning a path down my throat. I felt a profound sense of being undermined, a deep, internal churning that made my hands tremble slightly as I watched them finish the song, a hollow victory shimmering in their eyes.
